name: Nike Brasilia 9.5 Training Duffel Bag (60L)

specs.Pockets: Zippered main, zippered side, interior slip pockets

whatWorks: The ventilated separate shoe compartment keeps sweaty shoes or cleats isolated from clean clothes and gear—a category-defining feature that competitors have spent years trying to replicate and that Nike executes cleanly on a $50 bag

60L is the right volume for a hockey bag load (helmet aside), a weekend trip's worth of clothes, or a full gym and sports kit—large enough for any single-day sports use without being oversized for gym-to-work commuting
The water-resistant bottom panel protects bag contents in wet locker room floors, rainy sidelines, and trunk storage without requiring a separate dry bag or careful placement

Nike's Brasilia series has been continuously produced for over 20 years with consistent quality—zippers, stitching, and fabric that outlast the typical 18-month life of cheaper gym bags at this price point
worthKnowing: 60L is generous for a gym bag but may be oversized for daily gym-commuters who only need clothes, a water bottle, and a toiletry kit—the Brasilia also comes in smaller 41L and 24L sizes
No organizational interior pockets beyond a single slip pocket—small items like keys, earbuds, and chargers go into the main compartment unless separated with a small pouch
The shoulder strap padding is adequate but not premium; carrying 15+ lbs for extended walks will fatigue the shoulder faster than padded strap alternatives like the North Face Base Camp

commonComplaints.title: Interior organization is minimal
commonComplaints.body: Beyond the main compartment and shoe bag, there's only one interior slip pocket. Gear that needs to stay separated (clean vs. used clothes, electronics vs. wet gear) requires additional packing cubes or pouches inside.

commonComplaints.title: 60L is larger than expected
commonComplaints.body: First-time buyers who chose this for daily gym use find the 60L cavernous for a typical gym kit. The bag looks half-empty for a standard towel-and-change-of-clothes load, which feels unwieldy in lockers.

commonComplaints.title: No sternum strap for backpack mode
commonComplaints.body: When carrying as a duffel with the shoulder strap, heavy loads shift and can roll forward without a sternum strap. It's not designed for long-distance walk carrying—intended for car-to-locker or car-to-field use.

verdict: The Nike Brasilia 9.5 is the right gym duffel for athletes who need a ventilated shoe compartment, durable construction, and brand reliability at a sub-$50 price point. It excels for sports like hockey, soccer, basketball, and gym work where the shoe compartment earns its keep every use. Travelers who want a duffel for weekend trips will find the North Face Base Camp Duffel's backpack strap system and premium weather resistance more appropriate for mobility and outdoor conditions.

realBuyers.quote: The ventilated shoe compartment is the only reason I need this bag. My soccer cleats stay separate from my change of clothes. I've owned four of these over the years.
Perfect hockey bag for everything except the helmet. Everything else fits with room to spare. Zippers have held up after two seasons without issue.
Good price, great quality, and Nike still makes this the same way they did 15 years ago. That consistency is worth something.

sources.title: Best Gym Bags of 2025 | GQ Magazine

metaDescription: Nike Brasilia 9.5 duffel bag review: 60L, ventilated shoe compartment, water-resistant base. Best gym duffel for sports and fitness under $50 CAD.
introText: The Nike Brasilia 9.5 Training Duffel Bag (ASIN B0BCGCDJCP) is a 60L sports duffel with a ventilated separate shoe compartment, water-resistant polyester bottom panel, and removable padded shoulder strap. Available on Amazon.ca under $50, it is the go-to gym bag for athletes who need to separate footwear from the main gear compartment.
